title: podman-remote ps --external --pod --sort do not work.
body:
Fixup the bindings and the handling of the --external --pod and --sort
flags.

The --storage option was renamed --external, make sure we use
external up and down the stack.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

The change to Pod over the remote API is breaking - the responses used to
always contain pod information, now they only do if a new parameter is
passed.

podman-remote --sort and --pod work
can be tested with `diff <(bin/podman ps -a --sort id --pod) <(bin/podman-remote ps -a --sort id --pod)`

A Pod parameter should not be used for the bindings see https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7223
@Luap99 Thanks, I did not see that.  Added a comment to the code about this.
@containers/podman-maintainers This is ready to go in. PTAL
